data.title: Tech stocks bounce back, Nasdaq 100 jumped 1.4% surpassing 25,000. Cadence Design Systems Inc. leads with nearly 10% growth.
data.description: On Wednesday, tech stocks made a comeback with a strong rally in software companies, pushing the Nasdaq 100 up by 1.4% to over 25,000 points, surpassing the S&P 500's 1% increase and the Dow's 0.7% rise. Cadence Design Systems Inc. (NASDAQ: CDNS) notably led the tech surge with a nearly 10% leap after exceeding fourth-quarter revenue expectations and predicting robust growth for 2026, supported by share buybacks and increasing demand driven by AI technology. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (NYSE: IGV) also saw a 1.8% climb, while Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR), a standout AI company, surged by 4.5%. However, not all tech stocks fared well, with Palo Alto Networks Inc. (NASDAQ: PANW) dropping by 6% following a reduction in its annual profit forecast, despite reporting quarterly results above forecasts. The company's shares fell to levels last observed on April 8, 2025, during the peak of the Trump tariff uncertainty. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Merck partners with Mayo Clinic to use AI in drug discovery, focusing on tailored therapies for diseases like IBD, atopic dermatitis, and MS. Mayo Clinic's data will validate AI models, with Merck trading higher, up 45.96% recently.
data.description: Merck & Co. Inc. (MRK) has partnered with Mayo Clinic to utilize artificial intelligence and advanced analytics in drug discovery. Mayo Clinic, recognized as the world's top-ranked hospital system, will provide its extensive multimodal data to help Merck validate AI models and enhance drug development strategies. This collaboration focuses on high-need therapeutic areas like Inflammatory bowel disease, atopic dermatitis, and multiple sclerosis. Merck's stock is performing well, trading 6.1% above its 20-day SMA and 11.4% above its 100-day SMA, growing by 45.96% recently. This partnership marks Mayo Clinic's first major collaboration with a global biopharmaceutical company, showcasing the merging of clinical data with AI capabilities to improve therapy development.

data.title: Research suggests Treasury bills as main influence on Bitcoin's price. Not all new money affects risk assets.
data.description: Recent research conducted by Keyrock has revealed that the creation of new money does not uniformly impact risk assets, as the flow of fresh liquidity differs in its effects on the economy. Keyrock's findings suggest that not all newly generated money influences risk assets in the same way, highlighting the complexities of liquidity dynamics.

data.title: Carvana stock surging ahead of Q4 earnings. Analysts predict $5.23B revenue, $1.12 EPS. Company aims to sell more vehicles.
data.description: Carvana Co (NYSE: CVNA) stock is surging as investors prepare for its upcoming fourth-quarter earnings report. Analysts are predicting Carvana will announce fourth-quarter revenue of $5.23 billion, a significant increase from $3.55 billion in the same period last year. The company has consistently exceeded revenue expectations for the past seven quarters. On the earnings front, analysts are forecasting fourth-quarter EPS of $1.12, up from 56 cents a year ago. Carvana has surpassed profit projections in eight of the last ten quarters. In the third quarter, Carvana experienced 55% year-over-year revenue growth and sold 155,941 vehicles. The company is aiming to continue its success in the upcoming report.

data.title: Todd Emerson has been named President of Harry Norman Realtors. Atlanta-based firm, 96 years old, with Emerson serving for 21 years.
data.description: In Minneapolis, HomeServices of America revealed that Todd Emerson is now President of Harry Norman Realtors, taking over from CEO Jenni Bonura. The Atlanta-headquartered real estate brokerage, with 96 years of history, is well-regarded nationwide. Emerson, who has been with Harry Norman for over 21 years, previously held the position of General Manager. This transition marks a significant change in leadership for the esteemed organization.

data.title: Eli Lilly's trial finds Taltz-Zepbound combo effective in treating psoriasis and obesity. 27.1% achieved complete skin clearance and 10% weight loss.
data.description: Eli Lilly and Co. released results from a Phase 3b trial showing that the combination of Taltz and Zepbound was more effective in treating psoriasis and obesity compared to Taltz alone. 27.1% of participants on the combination reached complete skin clearance and a 10% weight loss, while only 5.8% of those on Taltz alone achieved the same. The combination also led to a 40% increase in patients reaching complete skin clearance compared to Taltz alone. Common side effects for the combination treatment included nausea, diarrhea, and constipation, while for Taltz alone, the side effects were mainly injection site reactions and dosing errors. The study's full results are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Chinese firm Laurore Ltd. invests $436 million into BlackRock's Bitcoin ETF, suspected as capital flight into crypto.
data.description: Jeff Park, an advisor at Bitwise Invest, suggests that China may be experiencing an influx of capital into the cryptocurrency market. Recent 13F filings reveal Laurore Ltd., a new entity based in Hong Kong, as a significant holder of BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust (NASDAQ: IBIT). The individual behind the filing, bearing the name "Zhang Hui," is speculated to be of Chinese origin. Laurore Ltd. lacks an online presence or public history and is believed to be structured in a manner typical of offshore entities accessing U.S. markets, possibly operating from the Cayman Islands or British Virgin Islands. Interestingly, the firm's reported holdings consist of just one asset. For more details, the full article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Big Spaceship reverts to original name from SPCSHP, naming Steve Street as Chief Creative Officer. Reaffirms global creative stance.
data.description: In a recent update on Feb. 18, 2026, Big Spaceship, a creative agency, revealed the promotion of Steve Street to Chief Creative Officer. This shift comes as the agency reverts back to its original name from the abbreviated version SPCSHP. The move signifies a reinvigorated direction for the agency, emphasizing its commitment to creativity and innovation.
